content: For teams building practical first-generation AI agents for end-user use cases, integrating intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) or random network distillation (RND) from curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can create personal assistant agents that autonomously learn new end-user workflows instead of only relying on pre-built tool integrations. A curious production agent would proactively explore under-utilized tools and unscripted user needs to expand its capabilities over time, without requiring explicit engineering updates for every new task.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
